coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: §3.2: 'All invoices are payable in U.S. dollars and are due net sixty (60) days from the date of invoice.'
§3.3: 'Any amounts not paid when due will accrue interest at the rate of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month'
§3.4: suspension permitted after 30 days overdue with 10 days' prior notice
No provision addressing customer dispute-withholding rights on invoiced amounts
No set-off or recoupment provision
SaaS subscription agreement; back-charges not applicable to this contract type
No most-favored-customer or price-protection clause

§3.5: 'Lattice may increase the Fees applicable to a Renewal Term by providing Customer with written notice (which may be by email) at least sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then-current Subscription Term.'
No customer audit rights for billing verification or compliance
§3.1: 'Fees do not include any taxes... and Customer is responsible for payment of all Taxes associated with its purchases hereunder, excluding only Taxes based on Lattice's net income.'
§3.2 states annual invoicing in advance but does not specify itemization, backup documentation, or format requirements
§3.3: 'Customer will reimburse Lattice for all reasonable costs of collection (including attorneys' fees)'
§3.2: 'All invoices are payable in U.S. dollars'
SaaS subscription; no construction-template or pass-through payment contingencies

Section 8.6: 'IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY'S TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT EXCEED THE FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CUSTOMER TO LATTICE UNDER THE APPLICABLE ORDER FORM IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE LIABILITY.'
Section 8.6 carves out '(B) A PARTY'S BREACH OF SECTION 7 (CONFIDENTIALITY)' from the cap, making confidentiality breaches uncapped as required.
Section 8.6 carves out '(C) LATTICE'S OBLIGATIONS UNDER SECTION 8.3 (LATTICE IP INDEMNITY)' from the cap, making IP indemnity obligations uncapped.
Section 8.6 does not carve out data breach or Security Incidents from the liability cap. Profile requires supercap of 3x or insurance coverage limits.
Section 8.6 does not carve out gross negligence or willful misconduct from the liability cap.

Section 8.3 provides only vendor IP indemnity. Section 8.4 imposes representations and warranties on Customer regarding Customer Data but no corresponding Customer indemnity obligation. Profile requires mutual indemnity for IP infringement and confidentiality breach.

Section 8.5(a): 'promptly notify the indemnifying Party in writing of any claim, provided that the failure to give prompt notice will not relieve the indemnifying Party of its obligations except to the extent the indemnifying Party is materially prejudiced thereby'

Section 8.5(b): 'give the indemnifying Party sole control of the defense and settlement of the claim (provided that the indemnifying Party may not settle any claim that imposes any obligation or liability on the indemnified Party without the indemnified Party's prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld)'
Section 8.5(c): 'provide reasonable cooperation in the defense, at the indemnifying Party's expense'

Section 8.7: 'IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR ANY LOST PROFITS, LOST REVENUE, LOST GOODWILL, LOST OR CORRUPTED DATA, OR COSTS OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES'
No liquidated damages provisions appear in this Agreement. Service credits in Section 2.3 are expressly stated to not constitute liquidated damages.

Section 8.6 carves out 'LATTICE'S OBLIGATIONS UNDER SECTION 8.3 (LATTICE IP INDEMNITY)' from the cap, meaning vendor IP indemnity is uncapped. However, there is no Customer indemnity obligation defined, so the question of whether Customer indemnity would be capped is moot in the current draft.
Section 9.2: GL at $2M meets Profile requirement. E&O/cyber at $5M meets Profile requirement. Certificate of insurance available on request as required.
Section 9.2 does not state that Customer will be named as additional insured on E&O policy. Profile requires this.

Section 8.3 final sentence: 'THIS SECTION 8.3 SETS FORTH LATTICE'S SOLE AND EXCLUSIVE LIABILITY, AND CUSTOMER'S SOLE AND EXCLUSIVE REMEDY, FOR ANY INFRINGEMENT CLAIM.' Section 2.3 final sentence regarding service credits. Section 8.1 regarding warranty breach remedies.

Section 8.6 carves out: (A) amounts owed under Order Forms, (B) confidentiality breaches, and (C) Lattice IP indemnity. The carve-out for 'amounts owed' is limited to Order Form obligations and does not swallow the cap. No 'except for breaches of this Agreement' broad language present.
No provision addresses whether the liability cap steps down to insurance limits or whether insurance is primary or excess to the cap.
Section 8.3: 'Lattice will defend Customer against any claim... alleging that Customer's use of the Subscription Services... directly infringes...' establishes defense duty for Lattice IP indemnity.
Section 9.2(a): 'commercial general liability insurance with limits of not less than two million U.S. dollars ($2,000,000) per occurrence and in the aggregate'
Section 9.2(a): '$2,000,000 per occurrence and in the aggregate'
Section 9.2(b): 'errors and omissions / professional liability insurance (which may include cyber liability coverage) with limits of not less than five million U.S. dollars ($5,000,000)'
Section 9.2(b): '$5,000,000 per occurrence and in the aggregate'
Section 9.2: 'Upon Customer's written request, Lattice will provide a certificate of insurance evidencing such coverage.'
Section 9.2 contains no requirement for Customer to be named as additional insured on any policy.
Section 9.2(b): 'errors and omissions / professional liability insurance' at $5M covers the professional services component (SOW-based Professional Services in Section 2.5).
Section 9.2 does not require workers' compensation or employer's liability coverage.
Cloud-hosted SaaS with no indication of vehicle use; auto liability is not applicable to this contract type.
Section 9.2 does not mention umbrella or excess liability coverage.
Section 9.2 contains no primary and non-contributory designation.
Section 9.2 contains no waiver of subrogation provision.
Section 9.2 does not require advance notice to Customer of policy cancellation or material change.
Section 9.2 provides for certificate of insurance 'upon Customer's written request' but contains no provision for renewal or annual delivery of updated certificates or endorsements.
Section 9.2 states coverage is required 'during the Subscription Term' with no post-termination survival period for coverage obligations or tail policy requirement.

Section 8.3 indemnity for infringement claims is uncapped (Lattice will 'indemnify Customer for any damages finally awarded'). Section 8.6 liability cap is '12 months of Fees' for all other claims. Cyber/E&O at $5M and CGL at $2M together provide $7M coverage, sufficient for most claims but indemnity exposure is uncapped. Cross-section noted but not a failure — insurance partially backstops the indemnity.

Section 2.3 states 'Lattice will provide the Subscription Services in substantial conformance with the service-level commitments set forth in the Service Level Schedule attached as Exhibit A.' Exhibit A not provided in contract text for review.
Section 2.3 cross-references 'Service Level Schedule attached as Exhibit A.' Exhibit A not provided in contract text for review.
Section 2.3 cross-references 'Service Level Schedule attached as Exhibit A.' Exhibit A not provided in contract text for review.
Section 2.3 states 'service credits described in Exhibit A.' Exhibit A not provided in contract text for review.
Section 2.3 references service credits in Exhibit A but does not state whether credits are capped. Exhibit A not provided in contract text for review.
Section 2.3 cross-references Exhibit A for SLA commitments; maintenance windows typically defined there. Exhibit A not provided in contract text for review.

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Acceptance criteria for Professional Services deliverables
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 2.5 states 'Each SOW will identify the scope of work, deliverables (if any), fees, milestones, and any acceptance criteria.' Acceptance criteria deferred to individual SOWs; no default acceptance procedure or deemed-acceptance period in this MSA.
Section 8.1(b): 'the Subscription Services will perform substantially in accordance with the Documentation under normal use during the Subscription Term'
Section 8.1(c): 'it will perform Professional Services in a workmanlike manner and substantially in accordance with the applicable SOW.'
Section 8.2: 'LATTICE DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT...'
No 'time is of the essence' or similar absolute-performance language found in contract text.
No means-and-methods control language found. Section 10.7 expressly states 'The Parties are independent contractors.'
No express provision relieving Provider from performance obligations when delays are customer-caused. Section 9.3 Force Majeure does not address customer-caused delay.

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Force majeure scope and SLA interaction
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 9.3 defines Force Majeure Event and excuses performance 'other than payment obligations.' No express statement of how Force Majeure events interact with SLA measurement (i.e., whether Force Majeure events are excluded from uptime calculation).
Section 2.3: 'Lattice's sole obligations and Customer's sole and exclusive remedies for failures to meet such service-level commitments are the service credits described in Exhibit A.'

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Right to terminate for repeated SLA failures
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 2.3: 'Customer's sole remedy in the event of repeated failures shall be the right to terminate the affected Subscription Service in accordance with Section 4.3.'

Section 8.1: 'Customer's sole and exclusive remedy and Lattice's entire liability for any breach of the warranty in clause (b) is, at Lattice's option and expense, to (i) modify the Subscription Services... (ii) provide a workaround, or (iii) terminate...'
Section 4.2: 'Each Order Form sets forth its own Initial Term.'

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Auto-renewal mechanics — length of renewal term
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 4.2: 'upon expiration of the Initial Term, the Subscription Services will automatically renew for successive one-year periods (each, a "Renewal Term")'
Section 4.2: 'unless either Party provides written notice of non-renewal to the other Party at least sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then-current Subscription Term'

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Termination for cause — breach, customer cure period
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 4.3: 'Lattice may terminate this Agreement or any Order Form upon written notice to Customer if Customer materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after receipt of written notice'

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Termination for cause — breach, vendor cure period
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 4.3: 'Customer may terminate this Agreement or any Order Form upon written notice to Lattice if Lattice materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within sixty (60) days after receipt of written notice'

Section 4.3: 'Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other Party becomes insolvent, makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or files or has filed against it a petition in bankruptcy that is not dismissed within sixty (60) days'
No termination for convenience provision appears in Sections 4 or elsewhere. Only termination for cause or natural expiration with non-renewal notice is provided.

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Definition of 'material breach' for termination purposes
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 4.3 uses 'materially breaches' without defining the term or providing examples of what constitutes a material breach.
Section 4.5: 'The provisions of Sections 1, 3 (with respect to amounts accrued prior to termination), 4.4, 4.5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10 will survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.'

Section 4.4(c): 'for thirty (30) days following termination, Lattice will, upon Customer's written request and at Customer's expense, make Customer Data available for download in a commercially reasonable format.'

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Post-termination fee obligations — early termination fees
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 4.4(b): 'if Customer terminates this Agreement other than for Lattice's uncured material breach pursuant to Section 4.3, Customer shall pay all Fees that would have been payable for the remainder of the then-current Subscription Term'
Section 10.1: 'This Agreement and any disputes arising out of or relating to it will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware'

Section 10.2: 'any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement... will be resolved exclusively by final and binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association... The arbitration will be conducted in San Francisco, California'
Section 10.2: 'The arbitration will be conducted in San Francisco, California'
Section 10.2: 'THE PARTIES KNOWINGLY AND VOLUNTARILY WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY'
Section 10.2: 'THE PARTIES KNOWINGLY AND VOLUNTARILY... WAIVE... ANY RIGHT TO PARTICIPATE IN A CLASS, COLLECTIVE, OR REPRESENTATIVE ACTION'

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Arbitration scope — carve-out for equitable relief
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 10.2: 'Except for actions seeking equitable relief, any dispute, claim, or controversy... will be resolved exclusively by final and binding arbitration'
Section 10.2: 'conducted... by a single arbitrator selected in accordance with such rules'
Section 10.2: 'Each Party will bear its own costs and fees in connection with the arbitration'

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Force majeure — defined events and effect
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 9.3: 'Neither Party will be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement (other than payment obligations) to the extent such delay or failure is caused by an event beyond such Party's reasonable control, including without limitation acts of God, natural disasters, war, terrorism, civil unrest, pandemic, government action, labor disputes, or failures of third-party telecommunications, internet, or utility providers'

coverage_pass_aggregate.item: Force majeure — termination right after extended event
coverage_pass_aggregate.evidence: Section 9.3: 'If a Force Majeure Event continues for more than sixty (60) consecutive days, either Party may terminate the affected Order Form upon written notice to the other Party.'
No provision in Section 4 or elsewhere grants either party a termination right upon change of control of the other party.
